data.title: Elon Musk's Tesla transfers $760M Bitcoins to other wallets - report
data.description:
Elon Musk's Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) has reportedly transferred almost all $760 million of Bitcoins (BTC-USD) that it owns to unidentified wallets.

The move has given rise to speculations that the electric carmaker might be selling the cryptocurrency.

Wallets which are associated with Tesla (TSLA) moved more than 11.5K BTC to other wallets, whose ownership has not been revealed yet, a CoinDesk report stated, citing data from Arkham Intelligence.

The Tesla (TSLA) wallets now only have about $6.65 worth of BTC, the report added.

The carmaker is the fourth-largest holder of the cryptocurrency among publicly listed companies in the U.S., according to BitcoinTreasuries data.

Earlier in March, a Morgan Stanley Research report suggested that since September last year, the historically positive correlation between Tesla’s (TSLA) share price and Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has broken down.

In 2022, Tesla (TSLA) had disclosed that it has sold 75% of its position in the cryptocurrency worth $936M. However, this was not the only time the carmaker liquidated some of its Bitcoin (BTC-USD) stash. The electric car manufacturer had sold $300M of BTC in Q1 2021 for what looked to be a decent profit.

BTC is currently trading at $68.2K.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures rise after Wall Street closes lower on tech sell off
data.description:
Stock index futures traded in the green, a day after chip stocks slumped on a disappointing outlook from ASML (ASML).

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.2% and Dow futures (INDU) were unchanged.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.01%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 2 basis points to 3.93%.

Wall Street closed in the red on Tuesday, as chip stocks took it on the chin following disappointing earnings from Dutch giant ASML (ASML) and news that the U.S. was mulling further export caps.

"Some rare weakness has come through in risk over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 falling back from its record high the previous day," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

ASML (ASML) shares were down 4% in premarket trade, after the firm said it expects 2025 net sales forecast to be between €30B to €35B (mid-point of €32.5B). The Dutch company also missed its third-quarter bookings estimates.

Morgan Stanley (MS) delivered a beat on both its top and bottom line in its third quarter, as banks continued to shine.

On the economic side, the September import/export prices and treasury buyback results are expected. The export prices index is expected to come in at -0.4%, while the import at -0.3% on a monthly basis.

data.title: Nel ASA reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Nel ASA press release (OTCPK:NLLSF): Q3 revenue from continuing operations was NOK 366 million, up 21% from the third quarter 2023 (Q3 2023: 303M). Revenue was positively impacted by delivery of alkaline electrolyser equipment whereas revenue from sale of PEM electrolyser equipment was low.
  • Net loss from continuing operation was NOK -115 million (Q3 2023: -167). The improvement was mainly explained by the NOK -90 million fair value adjustment from shareholdings in Everfuel in Q3 2023.
  • Order backlog was NOK 1 872 million at the end of the quarter, down 20% from the third quarter of 2023 and down 10% from the previous quarter.

data.title: Intel's products could face heat in China as cyber association calls for review - report
data.description:
The Cybersecurity Association of China has asked that Intel's (NASDAQ:INTC) products sold in China should be subject to a security review, alleging that the company has "constantly harmed" China's national security and interests, Reuters reported.

Intel's stock was down about 2% premarket on Wednesday.

The CSAC made several allegations against Intel in a long post on its official WeChat group, according to the report.

Intel did not immediately respond to a request by Seeking Alpha for a comment on the matter.

The organization, which is an industry group and not a government body, has close relations with the Chinese state. This could potentially trigger a security review from Asian country's cyberspace agency, the Cyberspace Administration of China, the report added.

CSAC said, "it is recommended that a network security review is initiated on the products Intel sells in China, so as to effectively safeguard China's national security and the legitimate rights and interests of Chinese consumers."

Last year, the Cyberspace Administration of China found that that Micron Technology's (MU) products had "relatively serious" potential network securities issues and warned that operators of critical information infrastructure should stop purchasing the goods.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Abbott Laboratories beats consensus in Q3, tightens FY2024 outlook
Scilex gains on spinoff plans for pharma subsidiary
data.description:
• Shares of Scilex Holding Company (NASDAQ:SCLX) added ~9% in the premarket on Wednesday after the pain therapy developer announced plans to spin off or seek a public listing of its wholly owned subsidiary, Scilex Pharmaceuticals, in the U.S. or overseas.
  • Scilex (NASDAQ:SCLX) said that its board of directors has greenlighted the plans as part of a broader move to maximize the value of Scilex Pharmaceuticals, whose portfolio includes three FDA-approved nonopioid pain products and a next-gen pain candidate called SP-103.
  • The Palo Alto, California-based company added that the potential value of Scilex Pharma could exceed that of its parent, and a spinoff or public listing would help unlock their actual values.
  • A potential strategic transaction and/or a dividend of Scilex Pharma common stock to shareholders of Scilex (SCLX) are also on the cards.

data.title: SANUWAVE Health to execute 1-for-375 reverse stock split
data.description:
• SANUWAVE Health (OTCQB:SNWV) is set to implement a 1-for-375 reverse stock split of its outstanding common stock on October 18, 2024.
  • The reverse stock split will reduce the number of outstanding shares of company common stock from ~1.18B shares to around 3.15M shares.
  • The common stock will continue to trade under the symbol “SNWV” on the OTCQB and is expected to begin trading on a split-adjusted basis at the opening of the market on October 18.

data.title: Wolfspeed in spotlight as Mizuho ups price target after funding 'lifeline'
data.description:
• Wolfspeed (NYSE:WOLF) was in the spotlight on Wednesday as Mizuho Securities upped its price target on the semiconductor company after it received a funding "lifeline" from the CHIPs Act and several private equity firms.
  • Shares fell 0.5% in premarket trading.
  • While the funding in aggregate addresses some of the concerns about Durham, N.C.-based Wolfspeed (including its roughly $5B in net debt), there are still headwinds, including a 10% to 20% decline in silicon carbide pricing; increased supply coming from China; and slowing electric vehicle sales, Mizuho analyst Vijay Rakesh said. Rakesh upped his price target on Wolfspeed to $9.50 from $8 but kept his Underperform rating.

data.title: Abbott Laboratories Non-GAAP EPS of $1.21 beats by $0.01, revenue of $10.64B beats by $90M
data.description:
• Abbott Laboratories press release (NYSE:ABT): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.21 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $10.64B (+4.9% Y/Y) beats by $90M.
  • Reported sales growth of 4.9 percent; organic sales growth for underlying base business of 8.2 percent, led by double-digit growth in Medical Devices
  • Abbott maintains its full-year 2024 organic sales growth guidance range of 9.5% to 10.0%, excluding COVID-19 testing-related sales.
  • Abbott now projects full-year diluted EPS on a GAAP basis of $3.34 to $3.40 and projects adjusted diluted EPS of $4.64 to $4.70 ($4.66 consensus), which represents an increase at the midpoint of the guidance range.
  • Abbott projects fourth-quarter 2024 diluted earnings per share under GAAP of $0.96 to $1.02. Abbott forecasts specified items for the fourth-quarter 2024 of $0.35 per share primarily related to intangible amortization, restructuring and cost reduction initiatives and other net expenses. Excluding specified items, projected adjusted diluted earnings per share would be $1.31 to $1.37 ($1.34 consensus) for the fourth quarter 2024.
  • In October, Abbott's board of directors authorized a new share repurchase program of up to $7 billion of the company's common shares.

data.title: China auto: BYD a top pick at Mizuho, big gains for Leapmotor and Seres
data.description:
Mizuho Securities called BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF) its top pick in the automobile sector after taking in the September sales reports from China. BYD (OTCPK:BYDDF) stood out during what was an overall solid month for auto sales amid some local subsidy help for buyers.

"With a recording-breaking wholesales of 418k units while new orders reached 450k in Sep-24, we believe the company has a good chance to achieve total wholesales of 4mn in 2024," highlighted analyst Joel Ying.

BYD Company (OTCPK:BYDDF) had 34.5% market share in the China during September to easily outpace Geely, Tesla (TSLA), and SAIC GM Wuling. Leapmotor and Seres Automobile saw the biggest year-over-year sales growth marks for the month. Leapmotor, founded in 2015 and headquartered in Hangzhou, China, specializes in intelligent electric vehicles. The company has gained recognition for its innovative electric sports cars and advanced autonomous driving systems, which feature 360-degree vision and multiple driving capabilities. Leapmotor's product lineup includes models like the S01, T03, and C11, catering to various market segments. Seres Automobile, formerly known as SF Motors, is a brand of electric vehicles under the Seres Group, which is part of Chongqing Sokon Industry Group.
